summary: sensivity ships obfuscated payload, Windows persistence, and masquerading supervisor process
details: On every load of the package's main entry, launcher.js executes ensureAutostart(), which writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run value named 'OneDriveUpdate' that runs wscript.exe against OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs. The.vbs in turn launches 'node launcher.js' hidden, masquerading as a legitimate Microsoft OneDrive component. The launcher then eval()'s a 120 KB obfuscator.io-packed blob (server.obf.js, 590-entry rotated _0x string array + control-flow flattening), which is the actual program. A detached supervisor with process.title 'Runtime Broker' (impersonating a real Windows component) is spawned with windowsHide:true and forever respawns a worker child whenever it exits, also force-killing any other process bound to TCP 3000 before relaunching. console.log/warn/error are hijacked to silently drop lines containing 'program module loaded' and to scrub references to 'sens.node' and the install directory from user-visible output — anti-forensics concealment. Behavior is gated on a PowerShell loop that enumerates chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave for a MainWindowTitle matching 'YouTube' (sandbox-evasion dormancy). The shipped 6.8 MB Windows native module sens.node (no source, no binding.gyp) contains skeletal-tracking strings ('LeftHand','LeftFoot','Head','Teleport') and a 'License Accepted' gate — game-cheat indicators inconsistent with the package's self-description as 'Sensivity Control Panel'. The combination of registry-key persistence, eval of an obfuscated payload, console concealment, supervisor masquerade, and an opaque native binary constitutes a persistent backdoor against any installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
